Life in Albania is one of Europe's greatest untold stories, and in this Albania documentary, Amazing Global Discoveries takes you deep inside the country the world overlooked for decades. Once called the North Korea of Europe, Albania today is a land of stunning beaches, ancient mountain villages, and some of the most beautiful women on the continent.
In this travel documentary, you will discover the real life of Albanian people, from the morning coffee culture with more cafes per person than anywhere on Earth, to the evening xhiro walks where entire towns fill the streets. We explore why Albanian women are admired around the world, not only for their striking Mediterranean beauty but also for their warmth, loyalty, and the central role they play in family life.
You will also uncover the secrets this country has kept for generations: 173,000 concrete bunkers scattered across the land, the story of the first atheist state in history, the ancient oath of Besa that saved thousands of Jewish lives during World War Two, and the mysterious tradition of women who lived their entire lives as men.
We break down the real cost of living in Albania, where a one bedroom apartment in central Tirana costs as little as 280 euros a month and a full meal costs around 8 euros. With a homicide rate lower than France and crystal clear beaches known as the Maldives of Europe, Albania might just be the cheapest paradise on the continent.
From the turquoise waters of Ksamil to the dramatic peaks of the Accursed Mountains, from UNESCO cities like Berat and Gjirokaster to the world's best ranked dish tave kosi, this is real life in Albania like you have never seen it before.
